name: Tahini and Hazelnut Dip
recipeInstructions:
1. Reserving a few of the best whole hazelnuts for garnish, pan-roast the remaining nuts in a large skillet over medium heat until golden, about 5 minutes. Alternatively, toast them in a preheated oven at 350°F on a baking sheet for 5-7 minutes.
2. Rub the roasted hazelnuts in a kitchen towel to remove skins, then grind them in a food processor.
3. In a deep bowl, combine minced garlic with tahini.
4. Drizzle lemon juice into the mixture and beat with a fork until combined.
5. Slowly drizzle water into the mixture while continuing to beat with a fork. Add more than 1/2 cup of water if needed, until the consistency is slightly thicker than heavy cream.
6. Combine the ground hazelnuts with the tahini mixture, then add salt and stir until blended.
7. Cover and refrigerate for several hours to blend the flavors.
8. Serve garnished with the reserved whole hazelnuts.
Makes About 3 Cups.
